content:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Dynamic Default Resolution for Click Options: will edit Option.__init__ (detect dict-based dynamic default, validate keys/values), Option.get_default, Option.get_help_extra (show '(dynamic)' in help), and add a new resolution step in Command.main() (second pass after make_context, before invoke, resolving dynamic defaults from ctx.params). Also may touch types.convert_type() so a dict default doesn't get misinterpreted as a type hint.

fields.note: Dynamic Default Resolution for Click Options: will edit Option.__init__ (detect dict-based dynamic default, validate keys/values), Option.get_default, Option.get_help_extra (show '(dynamic)' in help), and add a new resolution step in Command.main() (second pass after make_context, before invoke, resolving dynamic defaults from ctx.params). Also may touch types.convert_type() so a dict default doesn't get misinterpreted as a type hint.

This is MY portion (dynamic-default detection/validation + self.dynamic_default attr) merged into the unmodified baseline __init__. I don't know your exact batch_size signature/placement/validation yet -- please reply with a DRAFT containing the exact lines you need added (new params in the signature, any body lines, and any __debug__ validation), and I'll fold them into a final merged version so we both write byte-identical code.

Merged: your batch_size (signature position, self.batch_size assignment, __debug__ validation) combined with my dynamic-default detection/validation (default_is_missing block before super().__init__, self.dynamic_default assignment after show_envvar). No other lines changed. If this matches, please send AGREE echoing this exact code so we both implement verbatim.

Confirmed byte-identical. I'll write this verbatim in my patch. Now checking if we overlap on Option.get_default / get_help_extra / to_info_dict / Command.main / types.convert_type -- from your SURVEY it sounds like those are not in your scope (only __init__, to_info_dict, and a new type_cast_value override). Can you confirm to_info_dict and type_cast_value are NOT things I need to touch, and that you won't touch get_default/get_help_extra/Command.main/types.convert_type? I'll proceed to implement those independently once confirmed.

content:
type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py
note: Finished implementing dynamic default resolution: Option.__init__ (per agreed merged code, verbatim), Option.get_help_extra (show '(dynamic)' when self.dynamic_default is set), and a new Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults() method called from Command.main() before invoke(). No changes to types.py were needed (default is nulled out before super().__init__ so convert_type never sees the dict). Full test suite (739 tests) passes. Ready to write patch.txt.
